title: After Making a Decision: - Be Honest: Reflect on Whether Your Choice Truly Delivered
subtitle: Spot Choice-Supportive Bias
description:
After making a decision:
- Be honest: Reflect on whether your choice truly delivered the results you hoped for.
- Compare alternatives: Consider how other options might have played out.
- Learn for next time: Focus on improving future decisions rather than justifying past ones.

Example: If you bought a gadget and it didn’t meet expectations, admit it instead of defending the purchase.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Clarify your expected outcome
howTo.summary: State what you expected from your decision in measurable terms. This could include time saved, frequency of use, or any numeric target that anchors your expectations.

howTo.title: Gather initial evidence
howTo.summary: Take a few minutes to observe the actual results of your decision. Note one positive, one negative, and one neutral observation to create a balanced view.

howTo.title: Define a measurable next step
howTo.summary: Choose one small action to take based on your observations. Make it time-bound and numeric, such as setting a follow-up date or measuring usage.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app. Use the link to access the app and ensure you track your decision-making process effectively: https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/choice-supportive-bias-check-in/
